premise: After my husband died, my mother-in-law and I kept our weekly lottery pool going with funds from his life insurance—I put in $50 every week for five years. When we hit $1.8 million, she cashed the ticket alone, bought a beachfront condo, and claimed I’d just been lending her grocery money; I produced bank statements proving the joint venture, sued for a constructive trust, recovered $920,000, and bought the condo next door to turn it into a grief retreat for widows with a plaque reading ‘Generosity Rewarded, Greed Exposed.’

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My MIL Stole Our $1.8M Win, Bought A Condo, Called It A Loan. So I Bought The On
